questions.question: Which country is the host of 12th edition of The World Games 2025?
questions.explanation: The 12th edition of The World Games is held from 7 to 17 August 2025 in Chengdu, China. The World Games are organised every four years since 1981 and feature sports not included in the Olympic programme. This edition includes 253 medal events across 34 sports and 60 disciplines. A 17-member Indian team is participating in five sports — Archery, Billiards, Roller Skating, Wushu, and Racquetball. India has been competing since 1981 and has won five medals so far — one gold, one silver, and three bronze. India’s only gold was won by Aditya Mehta in billiards in 2013.

questions.question: Bharat Forecast System (BharatFS) was developed by which institute?

options.label: A
options.text: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune

answer.label: A
answer.text: Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune

questions.explanation: India developed the Bharat Forecast System (BharatFS), an advanced weather prediction model. BharatFS improves extreme rainfall forecast accuracy by 30% over earlier models. It was developed by the Indian Institute of Tropical Meteorology (IITM), Pune, in collaboration with the National Centre for Medium Range Weather Forecasting (NCMRWF), Noida, and the India Meteorological Department (IMD). BharatFS is part of the “Make in India” initiative supporting the Atmanirbhar Bharat (self-reliant India) vision. The system enables localized forecasts useful for disaster management and agriculture at village levels. India is the only country running such a high-resolution global, real-time weather system.

questions.question: The Dardanelles Strait, that was recently seen in news, connects which two water bodies?

options.label: A
options.text: Aegean Sea and Sea of Marmara

answer.label: A
answer.text: Aegean Sea and Sea of Marmara

questions.explanation: Turkey recently closed the Dardanelles Strait to shipping traffic due to nearby forest fires. The Dardanelles Strait is in northwestern Turkey, linking the Aegean Sea and the Sea of Marmara. It separates Europe from the western tip of Asia Minor. It is named after the ancient city of Dardanus. It was once called the Hellespont, meaning “Helle’s sea.”

questions.question: Which institute has developed a new drug candidate, Compound 3b, to fight deadly drug-resistant bacteria?
questions.explanation: Recently, IIT Roorkee developed a new drug candidate, Compound 3b, to fight deadly drug-resistant bacteria. It targets KPC-2-producing Klebsiella pneumoniae, a World Health Organization (WHO) top-priority superbug. Compound 3b is a β-lactamase inhibitor that stops bacterial enzymes from breaking down antibiotics. It works with the antibiotic Meropenem, restoring its effectiveness against resistant infections. Preclinical lab and animal tests showed major infection reduction in lungs and safety to human cells. It marks a major step against antimicrobial resistance and boosts India’s role in global biomedical research.

questions.question: Where was the Khelo India ASMITA Football League 2025-26 inaugurated?
questions.explanation: The Khelo India ASMITA Football League 2025-26 was inaugurated by Smt. Raksha Nikhil Khadse, Minister of State for Youth Affairs and Sports, at Godavari Engineering College Ground, Jalgaon, Maharashtra. The league promotes women’s participation in sports and nurtures young female talent, especially from tribal and minority communities. It features aspiring footballers aged 13 and under, providing opportunities from first-time players to hidden champions. The initiative aligns with the government’s ‘Sabka Saath, Sabka Vikas’ and ‘Khelo Bharat Niti’ for nation-building and women’s empowerment.

questions.question: Which zoological park recently received a pair of Smooth-coated Otters from Surat after 20 years in a rare animal exchange?
questions.explanation: Recently, the National Zoological Park (NZP) in Delhi received a pair of smooth-coated otters from Surat in a rare animal exchange after 20 years. Smooth-coated otter is the only living species of the genus Lutrogale. They are found in southern and Southeast Asia, China, India, and a small population in Iraq. Habitat includes lowlands, mangrove forests, freshwater wetlands, large rivers, lakes, and rice paddies. They are the largest otter in Southeast Asia with smooth, short brown fur and water-repellent guard hairs.

questions.question: Which ministry has launched the State Health Regulatory Excellence Index (SHRESTH)?

options.label: A
options.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

options.label: C
options.text: Ministry of Women and Child Development

answer.label: A
answer.text: Ministry of Health and Family Welfare

questions.explanation: The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare recently launched the State Health Regulatory Excellence Index (SHRESTH) virtually. It is India’s first national initiative to benchmark and strengthen state drug regulatory systems using a transparent, data-driven framework. It is proposed by the Central Drugs Standard Control Organization (CDSCO). It ensures consistent drug safety and quality standards. It has 27 indices for manufacturing states and 23 for primarily distribution states, covering themes like human resources, infrastructure, licensing, surveillance, and responsiveness.

questions.question: Chief Minister Kisan Kalyan Yojana (CMKKY) was launched by which state government?
questions.explanation: On 14 August 2025, Madhya Pradesh witnessed a historic event for farmers as Chief Minister Dr. Mohan Yadav released the second installment of the Chief Minister Kisan Kalyan Yojana (CMKKY) 2025-26 in Mandla. More than 83 lakh farmers will benefit, receiving ₹6,000 per year under CMKKY, in addition to the PM Kisan Samman Nidhi Yojana benefits. The scheme was launched by Madhya Pradesh government in September 2020. The scheme has already transferred over ₹17,500 crore directly to farmers’ bank accounts till March 2025. The installment aims to boost financial stability for Kharif and Rabi seasons, support input purchases, and encourage investments in allied activities.

questions.question: Where was drone competition “Surya Dronathon 2025” held in August 2025?
questions.explanation: The Surya Command of the Indian Army, with the Drone Federation of India, hosted the Surya Dronathon 2025, a pioneering national drone competition. It was held in Spiti Valley at Sumdo, Himachal Pradesh, featuring one of India’s highest drone obstacle courses at 10,700 feet altitude. The event brought together skilled freelancers and Original Equipment Manufacturers (OEMs) from across the country. It aimed to promote indigenous technology and innovation in the defence sector, supporting “Atmanirbhar Bharat.” Phase 1 took place from August 10-15, 2025, and Phase 2 is scheduled to take place from August 20-24, 2025.

questions.question: What is “Ideonella sakaiensis” that was recently seen in news?
questions.explanation: Researchers have identified Ideonella sakaiensis, a bacterium that can degrade polyethylene terephthalate (PET) plastic. It belongs to the genus Ideonella and family Comamonadaceae. It uses polyethylene terephthalate (PET) as both a carbon and energy source, breaking it into harmless building blocks. These by-products can be consumed by I. sakaiensis and other organisms. It is discovered by researchers in Japan from PET-contaminated soil. It is Gram-negative, rod-shaped, motile, non-sporing, non-pigment-producing, and monotrichous.
